0024c0284321b7e1.summary: Discover the MOTAT collection like never before during this unique behind-the-scenes tour.
0024c0284321b7e1.description: This is a unique opportunity to explore the MOTAT collection from behind the scenes. Attendees will get an exclusive look at the museum's vast holdings, discovering items and areas usually kept private from the general public. Don't miss these special extra dates for an unparalleled deep dive into the collection.

021f448b54049758.summary: Dr Josh Emmitt details the daily life of a museum archaeologist, from digging into the past to challenging misconceptions.
021f448b54049758.description: Digging into the past, excavating new information and challenging misconceptions is all in a day's work of a museum archaeologist. Hear Dr Josh Emmitt, Curator Archaeology at Auckland Museum, as he takes you through his daily journey of discovery during this special talk for New Zealand Archaeology Week.

034e0f1e13de5956.summary: Commemorate ANZAC Day with dawn service, tours, performances.
034e0f1e13de5956.description: This Anzac Day, Auckland Museum hosts a day of commemoration featuring the Dawn Service, free guided tours, choral performances, and poetry readings. Entry to the museum is complimentary for all visitors on this day. The museum opens immediately after the Dawn Service.

0625a85ce3dcc2fd.summary: A captivating journey through time uncovering the secrets behind the iconic Clock Tower.
0625a85ce3dcc2fd.description: Join us for a captivating journey through time to uncover the secrets behind the iconic Clock Tower. This talk will explore the history and significance of this landmark structure. The event is hosted at the University of Auckland General Library.

07ddca6f970286a6.summary: A workshop exploring foundational Siva Samoa steps to support wellbeing, honoring connections to land, ocean, and ancestry.
07ddca6f970286a6.description: This workshop offers a nurturing journey into Siva Samoa, where movement supports wellbeing. Participants explore foundational Siva Samoa steps and their cultural meanings—gestures that honour connections to land, ocean, sky, ancestry, and community. Through soft hands, grounded feet, and mindful breath, participants experience how movement mirrors the natural world. Rooted in traditional practice, the session is open to all levels.

0b472d46885f049a.summary: Professor Judy Bowen will discuss the reliability, usability, and safety of technology embedded in our daily lives.
0b472d46885f049a.description: Technology is now embedded into every part of our lives. This lecture will explore critical questions regarding technology, specifically addressing what steps we can take to ensure that the technology we rely on is reliable, usable, and fundamentally safe for all users.

0c5a2aaa12aaa6f5.summary: Professor John Dudley will give an overview of photonics, detailing the interplay between basic science and innovation.
0c5a2aaa12aaa6f5.description: This talk provides an overview of photonics, exploring this enabling technology. It will detail the critical interplay that exists between fundamental basic science research and practical innovation within the field. Join us at the University of Auckland for this insightful presentation.

0ce3b2985b99250e.summary: Dr Deane Galbraith explores Io traditions beyond the Supreme God Debate.
0ce3b2985b99250e.description: Dr Deane Galbraith (Ngāpuhi, Te Rarawa), Senior Lecturer in Religion/Mātai Whairuataka at the University of Otago/Ōtākou Whakaihu WakaFew, presents a look into the study of Io traditions.

0ce9fae1b4d99995.summary: Panel discussion on diaspora communities navigating global tensions.
0ce9fae1b4d99995.description: The NZIIA Wellington Branch Student Committee invites you to a panel discussion about the impact of rising global tensions on diaspora communities. Featuring Dr Kate McMillan and Dhilum Nightingale, this event will explore how geopolitical shifts affect migration, identity, and cross-border connections globally and in New Zealand.

0d0d2141e1bc8a11.summary: Tāmaki Makaurau musicians Ersha Island perform an intimate concert to celebrate the closing day of the exhibition Dreaming from afar.
0d0d2141e1bc8a11.description: Tāmaki Makaurau musicians Ersha Island will provide an intimate performance within the gallery, celebrating the closing day of the current exhibition, Dreaming from afar.

0f1ea4588f7224e7.summary: WEN Outreach Workshop on earthquake safety for young women.
0f1ea4588f7224e7.description: A free half-day workshop for Year 9 and 10 female students focusing on earthquake preparedness through creative problem-solving activities in engineering. Includes a complimentary pizza lunch.

1092d277ff445319.summary: This seminar explores building Aotearoa's future using data, featuring Databricks and insights from Tech Women.
1092d277ff445319.description: Deloitte Techweek26 hosts "Deloitte Presents: Perspectives in data: Building Aotearoa's future with Databricks and Tech Women" at Deloitte Auckland. The event takes place on Monday, May 18th, 2026, at Level 20/01 Queen Street, Auckland CBD.

11dc8475abd89d26.summary: Tamariki Time workshop at Te Papa, making nature picture frames.
11dc8475abd89d26.description: Engage in creative activities at Tamariki Time: Slow Burn – Ahi Tāmau. Create your own nature picture frame using native plants from Bush City.

184d6aaa593d1460.summary: Professor Alexandra Andhov discusses 'Big Tech: Too Big To Fail?' in her inaugural lecture.
184d6aaa593d1460.description: Inaugural Professorial Lecture by Alexandra Andhov addressing the role and impact of big tech companies. The discussion delves into whether these entities are too large to fail, challenging the status quo of digital dominance.

189671d66f52d283.summary: Explore siapo-making as a living practice through talanoa.
189671d66f52d283.description: This two-day workshop introduces participants to the traditional art practice of siapo-making as a living practice. Through talanoa, we will explore the history of siapo in Sāmoa, its cultural significance and the ways it continues to be reimagined today.

19b443dab561d5b4.summary: Celebrate New Zealand Sign Language Week with family-friendly activities at Te Papa’s marae on Rongomaraeroa.
19b443dab561d5b4.description: Celebrate New Zealand Sign Language Week with whānau-friendly activities. These special events will take place on Rongomaraeroa, located at Te Papa’s marae, providing a wonderful opportunity for the whole whānau to engage with NZSL.

1e871946e014fdb7.summary: Celebrate the announcement of the 2026 Ockham New Zealand Book Awards longlist, featuring readings from authors in all four categories.

1f6536ed97b55103.summary: 2026 WSBG Climate Festival Fortnight at Victoria University
1f6536ed97b55103.description: Wellington School of Business and Government (WSBG) at Te Herenga Waka—Victoria University of Wellington supports Wellington City Council’s first climate festival. Register here for more information.

2006b7e3c699fb83.summary: Professor Jonathan Barrett discusses respecting equal human dignity, focusing on taxation and commercial law in the human rights state.
2006b7e3c699fb83.description: Respecting equal human dignity: taxation and commercial law in the human rights state. Professor Barrett’s research concerns the practical application of human rights, particularly...

200da7812a57519d.summary: Lucy O’Hagan performs readings from her acclaimed memoir, Everything But the Medicine: A Doctor’s Tale.
200da7812a57519d.description: Lucy O’Hagan brings her acclaimed memoir, Everything But the Medicine: A Doctor’s Tale, to life through a reading and performance. This event offers attendees a chance to engage deeply with the book's narrative and themes through O'Hagan's captivating storytelling.

212868d681362f71.summary: Explore Yongchun lacquer baskets and paint your own.
212868d681362f71.description: Discover the beauty of Yongchun Lacquer Baskets, a traditional craft from Fujian blending weaving and lacquer art. Learn about its cultural story and create your own lacquer basket under expert guidance.

21be6b77ec2e071a.summary: Examines Southeast Asia's critical juncture amid global fragmentation, covering topics like the Iran War, Trump's tariffs, and the South China Sea.
21be6b77ec2e071a.description: As world order fragments, Southeast Asia finds itself at a critical juncture. The lecture will explore the impact of President Trump’s sweeping tariff agenda and transactional approach to allies.

22b6c643fdffc426.summary: Professor Papaarangi Reid presents on the progress and ongoing challenges related to Māori wellbeing and health equity.
22b6c643fdffc426.description: This session, featuring Professor Papaarangi Reid, will explore the current state of Māori wellbeing. The discussion will cover the progress made alongside the persistent challenges facing Māori health equity.

22f56ba953bbd60d.summary: Professor Jonathan Barrett presents his inaugural lecture on the practical application of human rights in taxation and commercial law within a human rights state.

speakers.name: Professor Jonathan Barrett
speakers.bio: Professor at Victoria University, focusing on the practical application of human rights.

2411337464843d25.summary: Explore Samoan female tattooing as cultural knowledge archive in a workshop by Grounded Rosanna.
2411337464843d25.description: This workshop centres the malu, the Samoan female tattoo, as a living archive of cultural knowledge and ancestral presence. Through discussion, drawing, writing, and embodied practice, participants will explore how Indigenous knowledge is encoded in the body, and how these markings intersect with gender, identity, and sovereignty. Grounded Rosanna's own malu and artistic practice inform the workshop's critical reflection on issues of cultural appropriation, authorship, and the responsibilities of working with Indigenous visual systems.

274567a260b0d69b.summary: An introductory course for complete beginners to experience Thailand's rich culture through learning the Thai language.
274567a260b0d69b.description: Thailand, often referred to as “The Land of Smiles,” is known for its rich cultural heritage, striking temples, world-renowned cuisine, and welcoming communities. Learning Thai provides a meaningful way to experience this culture more deeply, whether for travel, personal interest, or future engagement with Thailand. This introductory course is designed for complete beginners and those preparing to travel to Thailand.

27acf592ab1ec661.summary: Conference exploring critical reflections on development practices.
27acf592ab1ec661.description: This year’s theme, Rebalancing Worlds – Reworking Practice, invites us to reflect critically on development practice and explore pathways to more just and equitable futures in the field of international development. Organized by Victoria University's DEVNET program.

283045f10f790cd0.summary: Deloitte Techweek26 presents an examination of the future of Open Insurance within Aotearoa New Zealand.
283045f10f790cd0.description: Deloitte Techweek26 presents: The Future of Open Insurance in Aotearoa New Zealand. The event will be held at Deloitte Wellington, Level 12/20 Customhouse Quay, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand on Tuesday, 19th May 2026.

29e57fcd6effffcd.summary: Queers & Wares is a market celebrating queer creativity, community, and connection. Browse unique handmade pieces, vintage clothing, and art from local artists.

2cb1e777f13f1b20.summary: This webinar explores rethinking 'normal' from the neurodivergent perspective as part of the 'This Thinking Life' series.
2cb1e777f13f1b20.description: This session is the fifth webinar in the ongoing 'This Thinking Life' series hosted by Massey. It will focus on the vital topic of rethinking what is considered 'normal' within the neurodivergent community. Attendees can join the discussion online for an insightful exploration of this theme.

2cff4af0fa8b324e.summary: Professor Hella von Unger presents research in education.
2cff4af0fa8b324e.description: Join the School of Education for a seminar featuring visiting Professor Hella von Unger from Munich. Professor von Unger will discuss her latest research findings in educational studies. The event includes refreshments and networking opportunities.

2eea4335227e348d.summary: Dr Stephen Skalicky discusses the intersection of literal, figurative language, and Large Language Models.
2eea4335227e348d.description: The School of Linguistics and Applied Language Studies hosts weekly seminars featuring a selection of visiting researchers as well as those from the. Presented by Dr Stephen Skalicky.

2ef8a4ebd6343108.summary: Mandarin Corner celebrates Mid-Autumn Festival with Chinese legends and traditions.
2ef8a4ebd6343108.description: Celebrate the Mid-Autumn Festival and explore one of China’s most beloved traditions. Discover the legend of Chang’e, learn about mooncakes and festival customs. Hosted by Victoria University's Mandarin Corner.

2f6c979a89a98d02.summary: An exploration of capacity mechanisms used to ensure electricity demand is met in markets dominated by renewable energy sources.
2f6c979a89a98d02.description: Authors: Shaun McRae and Frank Wolak. Abstract Capacity mechanisms are an increasingly popular tool for ensuring that demand can be met as electricity...

310f8cfdd19d4d16.summary: Explore generative AI, contaminated information, and rhubarb in this webinar from the 'This Thinking Life' series.
310f8cfdd19d4d16.description: This is the sixth installment in the 'This Thinking Life' webinar series. The session will explore complex topics including generative AI, the challenges of contaminated information, and the fascinating subject of rhubarb. Join us online for an engaging discussion on these contemporary issues.

3119f8036c8cc821.summary: A panel discussion exploring aerial mapping technology held at the MOTAT Aviation Hall.
3119f8036c8cc821.description: Join MOTAT for an evening panel discussion in the Aviation Hall. Attendees will explore the latest developments and applications within aerial mapping technology. This promises an engaging conversation for aviation enthusiasts and industry professionals alike.

3139979a01b04c3d.summary: This workshop introduces participants to the traditional art practice of siapo-making, exploring its history and cultural significance.
3139979a01b04c3d.description: This two-day workshop introduces participants to the traditional art practice of siapo-making as a living practice. Through talanoa, participants will explore the history of siapo in Sāmoa, its cultural significance, and the ways it continues to be reimagined today.

3755a9be782fce30.summary: Victoria's Mandarin Corner explores Tai Chi basics.
3755a9be782fce30.description: Victoria University hosts a Mandarin Corner session focused on Tai Chi, inviting participants to practice this traditional Chinese martial art. The event combines language immersion with cultural experience through guided Tai Chi movements.

39f8376b33a79ab2.summary: Archivists discuss new discoveries at the Alexander Turnbull Library.
39f8376b33a79ab2.description: Archivists and historians unveil recently catalogued items from the Alexander Turnbull Library collection, featuring rare photographs and correspondence. These findings shed light on New Zealand's past and contribute to historical research.

3aba32013f4fbb71.description: Fast Forward 2026: Transcolonisation! Semester 1 In the Architecture NZ article Transcolonisation 1990-2020 , Anthony Hōete charted how architecture in Aotearoa was negotiating the complex entanglement of colonial inheritance and Indigenous resurgence. He described a 30-year trajectory in which design culture shifts from bicultural acknowledgement toward a more assertive Te Ao Māori architectural project, seeking to reconcile European traditions with Indigenous spatial knowledge.

3dff430382117182.summary: Explore the intersection of Indigeneity and Artificial Intelligence in the inaugural webinar of the 'This Thinking Life' series.
3dff430382117182.description: Join the online webinar for the first event in the 'This Thinking Life' series. This session will explore the complex and vital relationship between Indigeneity and the rapidly evolving field of Artificial Intelligence. Attendees can gain crucial insights into this intersection.

40f85f794ad4a481.summary: This lecture explores what happens when disability is viewed as a powerful cultural lens rather than merely a problem to solve.
40f85f794ad4a481.description: What happens when disability is seen as a powerful cultural lens, not a problem to solve? Join fellow AUT graduates for this insightful Great Graduates Speaker Series event.

47b8c89e63f1e5a3.summary: A creative workshop using printmaking and mindfulness to explore the familiar paths that bring a sense of comfort and connection to our daily routines.

47bff4f0c2afd6c2.summary: Explore the intersection of Artificial Intelligence and communication in this webinar of the 'This Thinking Life' series.
47bff4f0c2afd6c2.description: This online webinar is the third session in the 'This Thinking Life' series. It will examine the increasingly complex relationship between Artificial Intelligence and modern methods of communication. Participants will gain insight into how AI is reshaping how we interact and communicate in the digital age.

481fca738bd99f86.summary: Mark the closing of the He Kaupapa Waka exhibition with an afternoon of talks and a closing ceremony.
481fca738bd99f86.description: Join us to mark the closing of the He Kaupapa Waka exhibition. Attendees can look forward to an afternoon featuring talks and a rare opportunity to attend the closing ceremony, commemorating this important cultural display.

4dfb6447940ce651.summary: This lecture explores governance and operating experience across various regulatory environments, examining New Zealand's energy trilemma.
4dfb6447940ce651.description: This event explores the complex governance and operating experience across multiple regulatory environments impacting New Zealand's energy sector. Attendees will examine the core components of New Zealand's energy trilemma through case studies from the wider Asia-Pacific region. Hosted by the University of Auckland Business School, this discussion is central to understanding national energy security.

52cbd11960f48258.summary: Shaun Higgins discusses WWII POW art.
52cbd11960f48258.description: Curator Shaun Higgins explores how an Australian POW in WWII created remarkable portraits within a prison camp. Learn about the ingenuity and resilience of these artists during wartime adversity.

541dc5ec63ddc292.summary: Join Clinton Duffy, Curator of Marine Biology, for a guided talk before the Sharks exhibition goes overseas, featuring unique shark specimens.
541dc5ec63ddc292.description: One last time before the Sharks exhibition heads overseas, join Clinton Duffy, Curator of Marine Biology, and meet some of the denizens of the marine collections here at Auckland Museum. From the safety of your seat, dive deeper into the world of sharks with this guided talk, and even meet some of our most unique specimens like the Hammerhead Shark!

543b9068d04b75b5.summary: The Weaponisation of Economics and How we Might Respond: The Art of Economic
543b9068d04b75b5.description: The Weaponisation of Economics and How we Might Respond: The Art of Economic

5506b567e7f48ff1.summary: Explore ideas for a high-performing health system for New Zealand, featuring insights from global health policy expert Dr Ezekiel Emanuel.
5506b567e7f48ff1.description: This is an inspiring day for critical thinking and discourse, exploring ideas to create a high-performing health system for New Zealand. Keynote speaker Dr Ezekiel Emanuel, one of the world’s foremost health policy experts and author of Which Country Has The World’s Best Health Care?, will draw from his extensive comparative study of 11 nations to identify and discuss core components of world-class health systems.

575e90640f923cd8.summary: Join a cyanotype workshop to explore how science and creativity can coincide, guided by Lulu.
575e90640f923cd8.description: In this cyanotype workshop, participants will discover how science and creativity can coincide. Experiment with exposure, shadow, and UV light to create unique prints using natural materials gathered from surroundings. Lulu will guide two hours of playful exploration, learning, and good chats, perfect for anyone wanting to try art in a relaxed, supportive environment.

5b4464df305add17.summary: Professor Jennifer Miles-Chan delivers her Inaugural Lecture, exploring lessons found across metabolism, academia, and daily life.
5b4464df305add17.description: Professor Jennifer Miles-Chan will present her Inaugural Lecture, titled 'It’s the little things that count: lessons in metabolism, academia and life.' This lecture promises to draw insightful parallels between the processes of metabolism, the scholarly environment of academia, and the general rhythms of life itself.

5e031be2b6ffbc07.summary: Explore the art of Bengali embroidery in a hands-on, communal session led by textile and community artist Sahana Rahman.

speakers.name: Sahana Rahman
speakers.bio: Textile and community artist specializing in Bengali embroidery.

600ca550291d52a4.summary: Professor Nicola Daly discusses the underestimated power of picturebooks as reflections of what we want to pass on to our tamariki-mokopuna.
600ca550291d52a4.description: Picturebooks are often underestimated, yet they reflect profoundly what we wish to pass on to our tamariki-mokopuna. Professor Nicola Daly will explore the immense power held within these seemingly simple books. Join her to understand the cultural significance of children's literature.

61b44da4b6ece3fd.summary: Explore the fascinating world of uncovering history at a family-friendly event during Archaeology Week.
61b44da4b6ece3fd.description: Join us this Archaeology Week for a family-friendly event offering a unique opportunity to become an archaeologist for the day! Explore the fascinating world of uncovering history.

62204f2d9234daaf.summary: Mandarin Corner workshop on Chinese gold-foil burning art.
62204f2d9234daaf.description: Relaxed meet-up to practice Mandarin with native speakers. Explore the use of gold in Chinese art and create a simple gold-foil design.

62d42dd48958cee6.description: Hackathons offer a platform for students to engage in creative problem-solving with real-world applications. An AUT event for those interested in technology, innovation, and collaborative challenges.

6866a93602bd03ca.summary: Explore student life at Massey University Wellington's College of Creative Arts with workshops and panel talks.
6866a93602bd03ca.description: Experience Day offers a taste of being a student at Massey University Wellington's College of Creative Arts - Toi Rauhārangi. Join us from 9:30 am - 3 pm on June 12th for workshops, pizza, and student panel talks. Experience days are free to attend, but registration is essential to secure your spot on your chosen workshop.

69655c25a9a89e49.summary: Explore the intricacies of voting and elections as they occurred in ancient Rome during this 'This Thinking Life' webinar.
69655c25a9a89e49.description: This online webinar is the fourth installment of the 'This Thinking Life' series. It will delve into the complex mechanisms of voting and the electoral processes that shaped the Roman Republic and Empire. Attendees will gain insight into the political life of ancient Rome.

6a637e4eaaedf817.summary: The fifth edition of Printopia Festival celebrates contemporary printmaking across Aotearoa and the Asia Pacific from 1–3 May 2026.
6a637e4eaaedf817.description: Printopia Festival returns for its fifth edition from 1–3 May 2026, celebrating contemporary printmaking across Aotearoa and the Asia Pacific. Now a recognised regional arts event, the Festival presents its signature programme including the country’s largest print fair, live printing, artist talks and hands-on workshops. New for 2026 are curated exhibitions, guided gallery tours and experimental performances that expand how print is experienced. Taking place across four central Auckland venues, Printopia invites audiences to connect, explore and be inspired.

6c5fc1c057bcfa78.summary: Authors discuss writing from the margins and global publishing.
6c5fc1c057bcfa78.description: A conversation with three New Zealand authors exploring themes of writing from the margins, finding readership, and telling Kiwi stories in a globalised publishing market.

6c85bd14c67bf65d.summary: Neuroscientist Emeritus Distinguished Professor Cliff Abraham FRSNZ discusses how nerve cells in the brain facilitate memory formation and forgetting.
6c85bd14c67bf65d.description: Neuroscientist Emeritus Distinguished Professor Cliff Abraham FRSNZ will talk about what happens to the nerve cells in our brains to allow us to remember—or forget.

6cf49b2e5a77f75c.summary: AUT Worldwide takes over campuses from May 5 to 7, welcoming students from nearly 140 countries.
6cf49b2e5a77f75c.description: AUT Worldwide is back! Mark your calendars—AUT Worldwide is taking over our campuses from 5 to 7 May. With students from nearly 140 countries, AUT is hosting an incredible global experience.

6edac8ffc7e3fbf6.summary: Royal Society presents an urgent update on climate change.
6edac8ffc7e3fbf6.description: Join the Royal Society for a critical lecture addressing recent developments in climate science and their implications. Thin Ice 2026 aims to provide an urgent update on our planet's changing climate, highlighting key challenges and potential solutions.

6f04d15e54116813.summary: Doctoral candidates from Business, Economics, and Law compete in the Three Minute Thesis (3MT) Heat to showcase their research.
6f04d15e54116813.description: Doctoral candidates from the Faculty of Business and Economics and the Faculty of Law are invited to compete in the joint Three Minute Thesis (3MT) Heat. Present your entire thesis in just 180 seconds and take the challenge. This is an opportunity to showcase your research in a dynamic, compelling way and earn a place in the University 3MT Final.

6f8154369b41962d.summary: Professor Clare McCann delivers her Inaugural Lecture exploring the power and scope of speech-language therapists.
6f8154369b41962d.description: Professor Clare McCann will deliver her Inaugural Lecture, addressing the fascinating and broad capabilities of speech-language therapists. This talk promises to explore the many ways these professionals can influence communication and perception.

71fe220011968c3a.summary: Learn about scholarship applications for school leavers for the year 2027 at Victoria University.
71fe220011968c3a.description: Victoria University is hosting a session to guide school leavers through the scholarship application process for 2027. Attendees will receive vital information regarding available scholarships and the necessary application procedures. This is a key resource for students planning their post-secondary education.

7215d531111a9854.summary: The NZIIA hosts its AGM and Annual Lecture, featuring an address by Hon Todd McClay, Minister for Trade and Investment.
7215d531111a9854.description: NZIIA members are invited to the national Annual General Meeting, followed by the annual lecture. The AGM includes an address by NZIIA Chair Dr James Kember. The lecture features Hon Todd McClay, Minister for Trade and Investment.

7423082faeba0062.summary: Deloitte Techweek26 explores the balance between rapid development and incurring technical debt.
7423082faeba0062.description: Deloitte Techweek26 presents: Vibe coding or tech debt? Building fast without paying later. This event is hosted by Deloitte Techweek26 and will take place at Deloitte Auckland, Level 20/01 Queen Street, Auckland CBD, Auckland 1010, New Zealand on Tuesday, May 19th, 2026.

77b0a203c71c3323.summary: Canada's High Commissioner discusses Indigenous rights and UNDRIP.
77b0a203c71c3323.description: This meeting will start with an AGM followed by the High Commissioner’s talk on Canada’s approach to Indigenous rights, drawing from his personal and professional experience. The discussion will cover domestic commitments in a broader international context, focusing on cooperation between Indigenous peoples and governments, with special attention to synergies with Aotearoa New Zealand.

7879b554665913d7.summary: Screening of 'Welfare' (1975) with introduction and discussion on Aotearoa's welfare systems.
7879b554665913d7.description: This event features a screening of the film 'Welfare' from 1975, accompanied by an introductory talk and post-screening discussion led by artist Rangi White. The focus is on exploring and critiquing the bureaucratic structures within New Zealand's welfare system.

78c60ae414094338.summary: A quiz event at the Aotearoa Art Fair challenging participants' knowledge of art. The event is hosted by ArtSpace.

7bf84ac607a16c8c.summary: Dr Simon Robb discusses Regulatory Law through the Lens of Law-in-Context.
7bf84ac607a16c8c.description: The University of Waikato, with campuses in Hamilton and Tauranga, is a leading New Zealand university. They offer teaching and research across diverse disciplines, supporting a vibrant community of students, academics, researchers, and global partners.

7c69365a7120b66f.summary: Dr Oliver Hartwich discusses adapting to a tribal world in crisis.
7c69365a7120b66f.description: In today's complex global landscape marked by five interconnected crises—erosion of trust, decay at the institutional core, complacency about sustaining conditions, retreat from reason, and loss of Western confidence—Dr Oliver Hartwich explores adaptation strategies. He highlights these challenges as part of our current reality rather than future forecasts. Dr Hartwich is Executive Director of The New Zealand Initiative in Wellington.

7c6f453987ef3c34.summary: Experience the first screening of 'fugue notes' (2025) by Selina Ershadi, remastered for the London BFI Film Festival.
7c6f453987ef3c34.description: Join us for the first screening of fugue notes (2025) by Selina Ershadi since its remastering for its inclusion in the London BFI Film Festival. This film was included in the Anatomy of Place, Sites of Becoming section of the festival.

7fd7ba297f4c93f3.summary: Details For many generations, Aotearoa has invested comparatively little, certainly by OECD standards, in research and development (R and D). Moreover, since 2020–21,...

80a8f6a8a9efa1b2.summary: CreativeMornings Auckland returns after a reset period with a new organizing team, fresh themes, and inspiring speakers.

82e172e0631b1028.summary: Join a free, educational deep dive into Aotearoa New Zealand’s founding document, Te Tiriti o Waitangi.
82e172e0631b1028.description: To mark the signing of Te Tiriti o Waitangi (The Raukawa Moana sheet) on 29 April 1840, join us for a free, educational, deep dive into Aotearoa New Zealand’s founding document.

844b9ea53dc84eb6.summary: Mandarin Corner celebrates Dragon Boat Festival, crafts herbal sachets.
844b9ea53dc84eb6.description: Join our Mandarin Corner for a relaxed meet-up to practise Mandarin with native speakers. Celebrate the Dragon Boat Festival and craft your own traditional herbal sachet.

86b784eecf036925.summary: Explore the future of space in New Zealand, covering topics from Rocket Lab to developing a national strategy.
86b784eecf036925.description: From seminars and lectures to arts and sporting events, there are lots of interesting events happening at UC all the time! See what's on or search for an event.

87635656d44c1448.summary: Discuss the latest in AI-assisted coding at the Prompt Poets Society gathering at 2degrees headquarters.
87635656d44c1448.description: The cogs of AI progress keep a turning! Join the April 2026 Prompt Poets Society meeting at the 2degrees headquarters on Fanshawe St on April 28th at 6:00pm. Attendees will discuss and swap notes on AI-assisted coding in a collegial atmosphere, featuring prepared talks and open discussion. Pizza from Totos and beverages are provided.

87d8cabb11424c41.summary: Experience Chinese shadow puppetry at Victoria University.
87d8cabb11424c41.description: Experience the storytelling magic of traditional Chinese shadow puppetry. Watch a live demonstration, learn about its history and cultural significance.

8b30fa94d015ef93.summary: Film screening of Welfare (1975) with Rangi White's introduction on Aotearoa's welfare bureaucracies.
8b30fa94d015ef93.description: Artist Rangi White introduces a film screening of 'Welfare' (1975), directed by Frederick Wiseman, which explores the complexities and challenges within New Zealand's welfare system. The documentary highlights issues such as housing, unemployment, and medical problems faced by both clients and workers navigating bureaucratic laws.

93d3306be97bd05b.summary: Deloitte Techweek26 presents a talk exploring the idea that a customer may not be human.
93d3306be97bd05b.description: Deloitte Techweek26 presents: Your next customer may not be human! This event is hosted by Deloitte Techweek26 and will take place at Deloitte Auckland, 01 Queen Street, Auckland CBD, Auckland 1010, New Zealand on Tuesday 19th May 2026.

95282080ccc6256f.summary: Mr Frank Wilson discusses his experiences witnessing the Fall of Saigon in 1975 and its implications for New Zealand.
95282080ccc6256f.description: Mr Wilson will talk about The Fall of Saigon - 50 years On, drawing from his own experiences witnessing the fall in April 1975. He will also discuss the experiences of other New Zealanders there and the broader implications for New Zealand and the wider region. The talk is held at the Havelock North Club (8 Campbell Street) from 2pm to 3:30pm, including afternoon tea.

95802a3f9566c770.summary: The 2026 Alice Griffin Shakespeare Lecture explores the concept of Shakespeare acting as a reviser through The Merry Wives of Windsor.
95802a3f9566c770.description: This year's lecture, the 2026 Alice Griffin Shakespeare Lecture, will examine Shakespeare's role as a reviser. The focus of this scholarly discussion will be on 'The Merry Wives of Windsor,' exploring how Shakespeare's work itself suggests revisionary processes.

960eb2c54daf54c8.summary: Experience the show Hamlet, featuring content themes like blood, violence, and depictions of mental distress.
960eb2c54daf54c8.description: Hamlet’s looking paler than usual. Their father’s dead, mother’s remarried, and... The show includes content warnings for blood, violence, depictions of mental distress, and death (R13).

speakers.name: THEATRE 318 (Te Kāhui: Company) and 319 (Scenography in Produ

968071dd09d57105.summary: Printopia Festival returns for its fifth edition with expanded programmes.
968071dd09d57105.description: Printopia Festival returns for its fifth edition from 1–3 May 2026, celebrating contemporary printmaking across Aotearoa and the Asia Pacific. The Festival presents a signature programme including the largest print fair in the country, live printing, artist talks, and hands-on workshops. New additions for 2026 include curated exhibitions, guided gallery tours, and experimental performances.

97b507e8a2192a3a.summary: Professor Prasanna Gai explores shipping lanes, inflation risk, and optimal monetary policy.
97b507e8a2192a3a.description: Professor Prasanna Gai delves into the relationship between global shipping lanes and economic instability, focusing on hub shocks and their implications for monetary policy. This lecture is part of the Treasury's guest lecture series, featuring expert analysis from leading economists.

9c702e965742d928.summary: An online webinar discussing sounds and their historical implications as the final event in the 'This Thinking Life' series.
9c702e965742d928.description: This marks the final webinar in the popular 'This Thinking Life' series. Join us online for an engaging session exploring the connection between sound and history. This event offers a deep dive into auditory aspects of history.

a0102426b2daac5e.summary: Professor Rae Frances Irvine presents on new frontiers in chronic pain research.
a0102426b2daac5e.description: The lecture covers new frontiers in chronic pain research. Professor Rae Frances Irvine will present on the topic.

a244e0dfe41b06b5.summary: Join Ruth Buchanan for a tour of 'The Blue Dome' exhibition, exploring Selina Ersahdi’s commissioned films and installation.
a244e0dfe41b06b5.description: Join Kaitohu Director Ruth Buchanan for a tour of the exhibition The Blue Dome to gain insight into the newly commissioned films and installation by artist Selina Ersahdi. Ruth will discuss the development of the films.

a2e7b0f7f3d197cd.summary: Ceremony marking the conclusion of teaching programmes.
a2e7b0f7f3d197cd.description: This ceremony marks the completion of Victoria University's Graduate Diploma of Teaching and Master of Teaching and Learning programmes, celebrating the achievements of graduating students.

a38c1adb5dea7997.summary: A full day event for podcast producers, presenters, and practitioners from across Aotearoa and podcast enthusiasts.
a38c1adb5dea7997.description: Calling all podcast producers, presenters, practitioners and providers from across Aotearoa… as well as the podcast curious. Join us for a full day of co.

a616ebba62bce37b.summary: Webinar discussing NZ's $12B defence commitment and modernisation needs, featuring Major General John Howard and Sir Rod Drury.
a616ebba62bce37b.description: New Zealand has committed $12 billion to defence over four years, with a path to spending more than 2% of GDP within eight years. This webinar launches research on the need for institutional reforms beyond financial investment in modernizing defense capabilities. Speakers will discuss strategic areas like intelligence, space, domestic innovation and digital architecture.

a70971ae735940bb.summary: Learn basic communication in Bahasa Indonesia, the language spoken by over 250 million people across Southeast Asia.
a70971ae735940bb.description: Bahasa Indonesia, or the language of Indonesia, is spoken by more than 250 million people in Southeast Asia, primarily Indonesia, Malaysia and Singapore. Historically a trading language that linked people across maritime Southeast Asia, contemporary Indonesian is relatively easy to pick up for basic communication purposes, whether you’re planning a holiday in Bali or a business trip to Jakarta.

a7e028a3570d6069.summary: This workshop introduces participants to the world of Chinese martial arts through guided movements, cultural insights, and educational sessions.

aba91cecd08be353.summary: Victoria University hosts linguistics seminar on arguing about morphemes.
aba91cecd08be353.description: The School of Linguistics and Applied Language Studies hosts weekly seminars, featuring a selection of visiting researchers and local experts. This week's topic explores the nuances and debates surrounding morphemes in linguistic theory.

speakers.name: Prof. Laurie Bauer
speakers.bio: School of Linguistics and Applied Language

ac7d494ba6b6c5c1.summary: Explore challenging the patriarchy by reassessing ancient women's lives in the 'This Thinking Life' series webinar.
ac7d494ba6b6c5c1.description: This event is the seventh webinar in the 'This Thinking Life' series. Participants will engage with the topic of challenging the patriarchy through a reassessment of the lives and roles of ancient women. The discussion is designed for online attendance, fostering thoughtful examination of gender roles throughout history.

acb9de0bf9304fb7.summary: Explore redefining HR responsibilities in the human-machine relationship at the Human x Machines Lab.
acb9de0bf9304fb7.description: This session, held at Deloitte Auckland, focuses on the Human x Machines Lab concept. Attendees will explore the critical area of redefining HR responsibilities. The event addresses how to best navigate the relationships between humans and machines within modern HR and workforce decision-making processes.

adb78f3c44febf5d.summary: Join Dr Pilar Villamor for the annual Hochstetter Lecture, as she reviews 25 years of trenching active faults in volcanic environments and how a scientist's perspective on tectonics close to volcanoes changed through that time.
adb78f3c44febf5d.description: Join Dr Pilar Villamor for the annual Hochstetter Lecture, as she reviews 25 years of trenching active faults in volcanic environments and how a scientist's perspective on tectonics close to volcanoes changed through that time.

ae1b385a7cc1d662.summary: This year's Sir John Graham Lecture is hosted by the Maxim Institute in Auckland.
ae1b385a7cc1d662.description: Join the Maxim Institute for this year’s Sir John Graham Lecture. This event is being held at the Pullman Auckland Hotel & Apartments in Auckland. Attendees will gain insight into the themes explored during this significant annual lecture series.

af0a10e5eabd00ca.summary: Professor Derick de Jongh will discuss distinctive contours of African vs Western leadership theories, using Chief Albert Luthuli as a case study.
af0a10e5eabd00ca.description: Distinctive contours of African vs Western leadership theories: The Case of Chief Albert Luthuli. Derick's seminar will highlight a prominent leadership example from an African context.

b00b8f45e8db70bf.summary: Paula Morris and Pip Adam discuss the current state of New Zealand fiction.
b00b8f45e8db70bf.description: This session brings together authors Paula Morris and Pip Adam for an in-depth conversation about the current state of New Zealand fiction. The discussion will explore the creative process, intellectual depth, and determination shown by contemporary authors.

b06dbc473ad56b9a.summary: Learn basic New Zealand Sign Language greetings and introductions in this free 45-minute taster class.
b06dbc473ad56b9a.description: Join this free 45-minute taster class hosted by Victoria University. Attendees will learn some basics of New Zealand Sign Language, covering essential skills like greetings and self-introductions. It is an accessible way for newcomers to explore the fundamentals of NZSL.

b3e2988035940c65.summary: Dr Mahnaz Aliyar presents on the incidental acquisition of novel L2 vocabulary from authentic materials.
b3e2988035940c65.description: The School of Linguistics and Applied Language Studies hosts weekly seminars featuring a selection of visiting researchers as well as those from the. Presented by Dr Mahnaz Aliyar, LALS, Victoria University of Wellington.

ba2dd526b346cb10.summary: Deloitte Techweek26 explores how AI can be leveraged to build energy resilient value chains.
ba2dd526b346cb10.description: This event, hosted by Deloitte Techweek26, will feature a dynamic discussion on May 19th. Attendees will explore the practical application of Artificial Intelligence in strengthening energy resilient value chains. It offers a chance to network with innovators and change-makers.

bc36ce59b7e81181.summary: Learn the Japanese art of Kintsugi, repairing broken ceramics with gold to highlight their history.
bc36ce59b7e81181.description: This Public Record workshop will explore Kintsugi. Kintsugi, also known as Kintsukuroi, is an age-old Japanese art form. It involves repairing broken ceramics in a way that is often considered more beautiful than when it was whole.

be71cfa05e7f3e78.summary: A practical nutrition workshop designed to help Auckland University students maintain a healthy diet while studying on campus.

c1776f477373865c.summary: Artist Rangi White introduces a film screening of Frederick Wiseman's Welfare, exploring Aotearoa’s welfare bureaucracies.
c1776f477373865c.description: Artist Rangi White draws on the aesthetics of a Work and Income Office to consider how material and spatial conditions structure a person’s encounters with the state. The event includes a film screening of 'Welfare (1975)' directed by Frederick Wiseman, which explores the complexity of the welfare system.

c18c3e744bc93c82.summary: Explore Garrison World: Redcoat Soldiers in New Zealand and across the British Empire as part of the Between the Lines seminar series.
c18c3e744bc93c82.description: This seminar forms part of the 'Between the Lines: Recent Books in New Zealand Studies' series. The session focuses specifically on 'Garrison World: Redcoat Soldiers in New Zealand and across the British Empire.' Attendees can explore historical topics through this specialized lens.

c286231c9816d9f2.summary: Explore Indigenous psychologies, psychology, and the implications of Te Tiriti in the second 'This Thinking Life' webinar.
c286231c9816d9f2.description: This online webinar is the second installment in the 'This Thinking Life' series hosted by Massey. The session will delve into the intersection of Indigenous psychologies, contemporary psychology, and the foundational principles of Te Tiriti o Waitangi.

c2fc63ea6435fa7c.summary: Matthew Hanson will guide attendees through the artists and artworks at the Aotearoa Art Fair championed by Artspace Aotearoa.
c2fc63ea6435fa7c.description: Kaiāwhina Whakaaturanga Assistant Curator Matthew Hanson will lead an exploration of the artists and artworks at the Aotearoa Art Fair. These are pieces that Artspace Aotearoa has championed and collaborated with over decades. Please arrive at 11:15 am to receive your headset.

c3fe748234d3e3ec.summary: A free, interactive drop-in session for tamariki aged 5 and under to learn about sharing stories in New Zealand Sign language.
c3fe748234d3e3ec.description: This is a free, interactive drop-in session designed for tamariki aged 5 and under. Attendees will learn about the joy and art of sharing stories using New Zealand Sign Language (NZSL). It's an accessible and engaging way for young children to connect with storytelling through sign language.

c918880121cd1fe6.summary: H.E. Mr Lawrence Meredith, EU Ambassador to New Zealand, will explore how Europe and New Zealand are strengthening ties in an interconnected global environment.
c918880121cd1fe6.description: Co-hosted by the New Zealand Asia Institute and the Global Studies Occasional Speaker Series, this event features H.E. Mr Lawrence Meredith, EU Ambassador to New Zealand. He will explore how Europe and New Zealand are strengthening ties in an increasingly interconnected and contested global environment. His remarks will highlight the value of working with like-minded partners in the Asia-Pacific region to uphold open, rules-based cooperation and deliver practical outcomes.

ca1f64f253399828.summary: A special screening celebrating New Zealand Sign Language week, featuring 'Sign of the Times – The Story of New Zealand’s Visual Language'.
ca1f64f253399828.description: Celebrate New Zealand Sign Language week with this special screening. The film, Sign of the Times – The Story of New Zealand’s Visual Language, provides a look into the evolution and cultural significance of NZSL. This event marks a dedicated celebration of the visual language of New Zealand.

cce7967c19d96aa3.summary: A panel discussing the future of technology in Marlborough, hosted by Deloitte Techweek26 at Te Au Pūngao.
cce7967c19d96aa3.description: Deloitte Techweek26 presents a panel on the future of tech in Marlborough. The event will be held at Te Au Pūngao, located at Level 1/33 Arthur Street, Blenheim Central, Blenheim 7201, New Zealand. This takes place on Monday, May 18th, 2026.

ce1f249b3c34b63f.summary: Explore how Indigenous knowledge is encoded in the body through the lens of the Samoan female tattoo, malu.
ce1f249b3c34b63f.description: This workshop centres the malu, the Samoan female tattoo, as a living archive of cultural knowledge and ancestral presence. Through discussion, drawing, writing, and embodied practice, participants will explore how Indigenous knowledge is encoded in the body, and how these markings intersect with gender, identity, and sovereignty. Grounded in Rosanna's own malu and artistic practice, the workshop invites critical reflection on issues of cultural appropriation, authorship, and the responsibilities of working with Indigenous visual systems.

ced2707cbf21f28a.summary: Deloitte Techweek26 will present on Agentic blueprinting to help drive value from AI.
ced2707cbf21f28a.description: Deloitte Techweek26 presents Agentic blueprinting: Driving value from AI. This event takes place at Deloitte Wellington, Level 12/20 Customhouse Quay, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, on Tuesday 19th May 2026. Attendees should register on Humanitix for event information.

cee11c9c166f76f7.summary: Explore the world of sharks from prehistoric times to today in a theatrical journey. Dive into deep waters without the need for a wetsuit or breathing apparatus.

d92b21fc2b42ebf3.summary: International Research Symposium on work-integrated learning.
d92b21fc2b42ebf3.description: The International Research Symposium, organized by WACE, explores transforming practice through research in work-integrated education. This event aims to bring together global experts for a collaborative dialogue and innovation in cooperative and experiential educational methods.

dab41faa544e7e75.summary: This session hosted by Deloitte Techweek26 will explore demographics, productivity, and AI choices facing New Zealand.
dab41faa544e7e75.description: Deloitte presents: We can’t hire our way out of this - demographics, productivity and AI choices facing NZ. The event is hosted by Deloitte Techweek26 and will be held at Deloitte Wellington, Level 12/20 Customhouse Quay, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand on Wednesday 20th May 2026. Find event information to register.

db21f3bf1d770c7f.summary: Exploring societal challenges in New Zealand.
db21f3bf1d770c7f.description: In Aotearoa, key issues like poverty, inequity, ecological destruction, and climate change are well understood but require urgent action. This lecture delves into these problems and potential solutions for a just society.

speakers.name: Boyd Scirkovich
speakers.bio: Chief Economic Development and Investment Officer

speakers.name: Gareth Hughes
speakers.bio: Director Wellbeing Economy Alliance Aotearoa NZ

speakers.name: Bianca Elkington
speakers.bio: General Manager Education and Employment, Te

speakers.name: Fleur Fitzsimons
speakers.bio: National Secretary Public Service Association Te

speakers.name: Edward Miller
speakers.bio: Researcher, Centre for International Corporate Tax

speakers.name: Rebecca Kiddle and James Berghan
speakers.bio: Director and senior research fellow at

e1cb94aaa2fee747.summary: Victoria hosts a lecture on philanthropy in supply chains.
e1cb94aaa2fee747.description: Victoria University presents 'Philanthropy Along the Supply Chain' by authors Huu Nhan Duong, Emdad Islam, Lubna Rahman, and Cara Vansteenkiste. Abstract focuses on strategic use of donations to trade partners’ affiliated charities.

e2788c45a4a1bec8.summary: Professor Stefano Pampanin discusses the 15 years of earthquake engineering lessons learned from Christchurch.
e2788c45a4a1bec8.description: Building Back Better: 15 Years of Earthquake Engineering Lessons from Christchurch. Professor Stefano Pampanin will speak on this topic.

e32518eb218ec103.description: Discover how science and creativity intersect through cyanotyping. Experiment with UV light and natural materials to create unique prints under the guidance of Lulu, fostering a relaxed and supportive artistic environment.

e42257690cdde024.summary: A Techweek Appathon challenge hosted by Deloitte and ServiceNow for University of Canterbury students.
e42257690cdde024.description: Deloitte and ServiceNow are partnering with the University of Canterbury to host a Techweek Appathon challenge. This event challenges UC students to engage with cutting-edge technology in a hands-on development environment.

e48ddbbc834dc652.summary: Learn about Otago's Deep-Sea Research Programme, exploring the secrets of the Southern Ocean.
e48ddbbc834dc652.description: This session will delve into the groundbreaking work being done within Otago's Deep-Sea Research Programme. Discover the latest scientific insights gathered from the Southern Ocean.

e63364a32a4ba7ef.summary: Attend the NZIIA Auckland Branch AGM and hear Catherine Grant Makokera present on New Zealand’s Trade Policy Amid Global Conflict.
e63364a32a4ba7ef.description: Attend the NZIIA Auckland Branch’s Annual General Meeting for 2026 on Tuesday, April 28th, 2026. The AGM will cover the past year, thank the committee, outline upcoming events, and elect new committee members. Following the AGM, Catherine Grant Makokera will present on New Zealand’s Trade Policy During an Era of Geoeconomics and Global Conflict.

e7ee177f3796e430.summary: Explore the journey, history, and mātauranga surrounding the prestigious pauku cloak at Auckland Museum.
e7ee177f3796e430.description: The Auckland War Memorial Museum invites you to hear about the journey of the pauku, its history, care, and the important mātauranga surrounding this prestigious cloak. Join us for an evening at Tāmaki Paenga Hira.

e885f0a7f721ce37.summary: This event discusses Canterbury Water's focus on ecology, irrigation, and the future of freshwater.
e885f0a7f721ce37.description: From seminars and lectures to arts and sporting events, there are lots of interesting events happening at UC all the time. See what's on or search for an event.

e8cbee17043daa26.summary: Workshop on Siva Samoa movement and cultural significance.
e8cbee17043daa26.description: This workshop offers a nurturing journey into Siva Samoa, where participants explore foundational steps and their cultural meanings. Through gentle movements, they experience the connection to nature and community, promoting health and collective wellbeing.

ec949af9f84b83e4.summary: Explore the future of fitness and discover the innovative world of sports technology through a weekend filled with engaging activities.

f3fabdf5c39be407.summary: Artist Selina Ershadi and Kaitohu Director Ruth Buchanan provide an informal introduction to The Blue Dome.
f3fabdf5c39be407.description: Alongside the Aotearoa Art Fair and neighbourhood gallery openings, the gallery will remain open until 8pm. Attendees can join artist Selina Ershadi and Kaitohu Director Ruth Buchanan for drinks and an informal introduction to The Blue Dome and its newly commissioned works.

f575220e54475c4f.summary: An event series reflecting on the past to inform the present and envision the future of the Bachelor of Social Work qualification.
f575220e54475c4f.description: This event series celebrates the 50th anniversary of the Bachelor of Social Work qualification. Attendees will reflect on the program's past to inform current practice and envision its future trajectory.

f736386a033bed4f.summary: Helen Howell will discuss visual priming and speech perception among Samoan speakers at the School of Linguistics and Applied Language Studies.
f736386a033bed4f.description: The School of Linguistics and Applied Language Studies hosts weekly seminars featuring a selection of visiting researchers as well as those from the. This seminar is presented by Helen Howell, University of Potsdam.

f9522d3572d03fb2.summary: Future Environments Thought Leadership session, in partnership with Whaikaha – Ministry of Disabled People, focused on accessible and inclusive design in
f9522d3572d03fb2.description: Future Environments Thought Leadership session, in partnership with Whaikaha – Ministry of Disabled People, focused on accessible and inclusive design in

speakers.name: Rebecca Elvy
speakers.bio: Deputy Chief Executive Outreach and Innovation

f97d2adb031d11ec.summary: Mahdis Azarmandi presents a lecture offering insight into the complex context surrounding war and peace in Iran.
f97d2adb031d11ec.description: Inheriting Revolution—On Iran: the question of war and peace. Drawing from her research within Peace and Conflict Studies, Mahdis Azarmandi will present a lecture that provides insight into the complex context which plays a significant role in the region.

speakers.name: Mahdis Azarmandi
speakers.bio: Research in Peace and Conflict Studies

fdd4532b42c7f1b5.summary: Explore the Business Administration – Postgraduate Certificate in Business and its pathway to an Executive MBA.
fdd4532b42c7f1b5.description: Explore the Business Administration – Postgraduate Certificate in Business during this online information session. The program serves as a direct pathway toward an Executive MBA qualification.

fea2f6e6b34612a6.summary: Artspace hosts Ruth Buchanan for a live reading of her artwork and published collection, Evacuation Tapes.
fea2f6e6b34612a6.description: Kaitohu Director and artist Ruth Buchanan will present a live interpretation of her artwork and published collection Evacuation Tapes (2019). This spoken word work explores the construction of the self and the ways in which our bodies and their.

ff8ae22d11fe7fac.summary: Artists and filmmakers Selina Ershadi and Nova Paul lead a workshop for early career artists interested in moving image making.
ff8ae22d11fe7fac.description: Artists and filmmakers Selina Ershadi and Nova Paul lead a one-day workshop for early career artists with an interest in moving image making. This workshop will draw on Selina and Nova’s interest in film as a key tool to tell personal stories.
